questions.question: Explain the difference between explicit and implicit intents with suitable examples.
keyPoints: Explicit intent specifies exact component name
Implicit intent declares action without naming component
System resolves implicit intents via intent filters

questions.question: What is the Android Navigation Component? List its main components.
keyPoints: Navigation Component is a library for in-app navigation
Navigation Graph: XML defining destinations and actions

questions.question: Describe deep linking in Android and how to implement it.
keyPoints: Deep linking allows direct navigation to specific content via URI
Add deepLink element in Navigation Graph
Can use URI patterns for dynamic routing

questions.question: Explain the concept of Single Activity Architecture with Fragments.
keyPoints: All navigation happens within fragments via Navigation Component

questions.question: What is the difference between Up Navigation and Back Navigation?
keyPoints: Up goes to logical parent in hierarchy
Back navigation uses device back button
Back returns to previous destination in stack
Up defined via parentActivity in manifest

questions.question: Explain Navigation Arguments and Safe Args in Android.
keyPoints: Safe Args generates type-safe Java classes
Accessed via getArguments() or generated Directions class

questions.question: Design a Navigation Graph XML for an e-commerce app with categories, product listing, product details, cart, and checkout screens. Include appropriate actions and demonstrate how to pass product ID from listing to details.
keyPoints: Define actions: categories->listing, listing->details, listing->cart, cart->checkout
Add argument productId to productDetails destination
Use Safe Args to pass and retrieve productId
Include popUpTo for proper back stack handling

questions.question: Write a complete code example demonstrating fragment navigation using Navigation Component with bottom navigation. Include setup of NavHostFragment, navigation graph, and fragment switching logic.
keyPoints: Create navigation graph with 3 destinations (home, search, profile)
